title: Rolex “Full-Set 6239 Paul Newman” Daytona
description: Offered here is a lovely example of Rolex Daytona, Ref. 6239, circa 1968, fit with an exotic dial, nicknamed the Paul Newman Daytona, due to the fact that the famous actor favored wearing the Rolex Daytona with the exotic dial, and can be seen in many photographs wearing the watch. This example is outfitted with a tri-color reverse-panda dial and pump pushers. Further, this particular example comes with its original Rolex box, Daytona booklet, and Guarantee Certificate booklet, showing the origi

title: Rolex “168000 Full-Set Provenance COMEX” Submariner
description: Up for grabs is an incredibly rare Rolex Submariner, Ref. 168000, made for COMEX, circa 1987. COMEX (Compagnie Maritime d’Expertses), which stands for “Expert Maritime Company”, was a famous diving company which specialized in deep underwater engineering, construction, and exploration. Rolex partnered with COMEX in the 1970s, and supplied them with Submariner and Sea-Dweller wristwatches for their underwater exploits a collaboration that was unheard of for the brand to that point, and has yet to

title: Patek Philippe “3800A White” Nautilus
description: Offered here is a very nice and uncommon example of a Patek Philippe Nautilus, the Reference 3800, in stainless steel. These watches have exploded in popularity as of late, and are considered by many to be the best design of Gerald Genta. This mid-size 3800 stands out, due to the fact that it was born with a less common white dial, as opposed to the standard blue fare, as confirmed by the Extract of the Archives included in the sale (this example was originally sold in 1998). This Patek Philippe

title: Rolex “6263 Panda” Daytona
description: Offered here is a beautiful example of a Rolex Daytona, Ref. 6263 (circa 1972-73; 3.7m serial), nicknamed the Panda Daytona. This example is outfitted with a silver dial, and features screwdown pushers. These screwdown pusher-Daytonas have soared in popularity over the last couple decades, and are coveted by collectors, new and old. The condition of this Daytona is excellent. The case remains strong, albeit worn from use, overall well-preserved, likely very lightly polished in the past. The dial

title: Universal Geneve “Disco Volante 100108” Dioramic
description: Offered here is a very hard-to-come-by 1950s Universal Geneve Dioramic, reference 100108/1. These watches were produced in the 1950s in solid gold, and feature a large, disco volante style case, with an oversized bezel, and date function at 6 oclock, powered by a UG bumper movement. These watches have always been something of a novelty in the UG line-up, and most recently, Breitling has decided to relaunch the brand, offering the Dioramic model in a new 37mm case (up 2mm from the original 35mm).

title: Audemars Piguet “5403BC” Cobra
description: Up for grabs is an uncommon and increasingly popular 1970s Audemars Piguet Cobra, reference 5403BC. The AP Cobra is an 18kt white gold intergrated bracelet wristwatch, so-called the Cobra due to the serpetine-style finish of the case and bracelet. This particular example has been fitted with a blue sunburst factory diamond dial. Further, the condition of the watch is excellent, seemingly unpolished and with no visible wear to the case or band; the dial shows some light aging, noticeably around t

title: Rolex “Serif-Dial 5513” Submariner
description: Up for grabs is a lovely serif Rolex Submariner Ref. 5513, circa 1974 (4.2m serial). The serif dials were the 3rd iteration (after the meters first dial and the non-serif dial) of Rolex Submariner models to feature a matte dial, produced through the 1970s. This Rolex Submariner is in great condition. The case appears unpolished (albeit worn from use) the lugs remain thick and even, with original beveled edges visible. The dial is flawless, with no chips, scratches, tool marks, etc. The original

title: Rolex “1503 14kt Yellow Gold” Date
description: Offered here is a crisp Rolex Date model, Reference 1503, housed in 14kt Yellow Gold. This particular example has a serial of 5.9m, and dates to circa 1979. The watch itself is in immaculate condition. The case is unpolished and with minimal wear. The dial is mint, with no chips, scratches, marks, etc. The original tritium lume is present on both dial and hands, aged to a vibrant yellow color.

title: Rolex “Full-Set Beach 116519” Daytona
description: Offered here is a rare and exceedingly popular Rolex Daytona, in 18kt white gold, reference 116519, circa 2001. This particular Rolex Daytona is part of the famous Beach line, produced by Rolex in the early 2000s, in four variations (green, blue, yellow and pink). This particular example was fitted with a green chrysoprase dial (which is a chalcedony gemstone), and matching green strap. Further, this particular example remains complete with its original Rolex beach green inner and outer box (a h

title: Movado “M95 Sector 19038” Chronograph
description: Up for grabs is a lovely example of a Movado chronograph, circa 1950s, featuring their legendary M95 movement. This example stands out with a gorgeous white sector dial, with the Movado signature at 6 oclock, a variant we had not seen before. The case is in excellent shape, and is unpolished and sharp. The dial is mint, with no major flaws.

title: Audemars Piguet “Maw Sit Sit” Watch
description: Up for grabs is an exceedingly rare 1970s Audemars Piguet watch in 18kt yellow gold; to date, we can only find one other example the same as ours. This particular watch was fit with a very uncommon Maw Sit Sit stone dial, which is a green gemstone found in northern Burma, often confused with Jade. Further, this crazy rare Audemars Piguet remains in excellent vintage condition, remaining unpolished with sharp engravings, and a perfect, crack-free stone dial.

title: Patek Philippe “3700/11J” Nautilus
description: Offered here is a lovely example of a Patek Philippe Jumbo Nautilus, the Reference 3700/11J in solid 18kt yellow gold, circa 1982-1983. These watches have exploded in popularity as of late, and are considered by many to be the best design of Gerald Genta. Further, the present example is a desirable /11 reference, with a more tapered bracelet as compared to its /1 predecessor. This Patek Phillipe Nautilus is in great vintage condition. The case has been previously polished, but still shows nicely

title: Patek Philippe “5040R” Perpetual Calendar
description: Offered here is a very beautiful Patek Philippe Perpetual Calendar watch, reference 5040R, in rose gold. While not a rare reference in-and-of itself, finding the 5040R in 18kt rose gold can be difficult, as most are in yellow gold or platinum. The reference 5040 was introduced by Patek Philippe in 1992, and is essentially a 3940 in a tonneau-shaped case; while the 3940 has exploded in popularity, the 5040 has remained overlooked, and represents tremendous value in the perpetual calendar Patek wo

title: Patek Philippe “Art Deco 3406J” Square Watch
description: Offered here is a super funky and interesting Patek Philippe watch, given the reference 3406, and made in 18kt yellow gold. This particular watch was produced and sold in 1959 (according to the Extract of the Archives, which is included in the sale), and produced in a nicely sized 27mm x 38mm case with a cool Art-Deco-style applied indices dial. The watch itself remains in nice, unrestored condition, with a seemingly unpolished case with strong hallmarks, and original dial showing light patina/a

title: Patek Philippe “Full-Set 5035G” Annual Calendar
description: Offered here is a stunning Patek Philippe Annual Calendar, reference 5035G in 18kt white gold. The reference 5035G is a landmark timepiece that redefined modern complications by introducing the world’s first patented annual calendar when it debuted in 1996. This particular example fitted with a lovely and popular slate grey dial is in excellent condition, clearly sparingly used. Further, this 5035G comes complete, with its original Patek Philippe box, Certificate of Origin, booklets, hangtag, an

title: Jacob & Co. Astronomia Casino “Black Gold”
description: Offered here is an absolutely wild example of one of the most audacious watches ever produced — the Jacob & Co. Astronomia Casino Black. This is not a watch for the faint of heart. The Astronomia already features four rotating satellite arms carry an off-centred hours and minutes dial, a 288-facet 1-carat Jacob-cut diamond spinning on itself every 60 seconds, a magnesium Earth globe also rotating every 60 seconds, and a double-axis flying tourbillon completing its first axis in 60 seconds and it

title: Parmigiani Fleurier Tonda PF Micro-Rotor “BNIB Serenissima” x Revolution & Rake
description: Up for grabs is one of the most underrated independent watch brands of recent years, Parmigiani Fleurier. This particular model, the Tonda PF Micro-Rotor was made in collaboration with none other than Wei Koh from Revolution and The Rake. This watch features a hand-guilloche dial in a lovely salmon color. Serenissima, or serenity in english was the inspiration for this dial color, as Wei described it as recalling Botticelli painting the first light rising over the Gulf of Venice at the north of

title: Kudoke 1
description: Offered here is a very rare and beautiful watch from the German independent outfit, Kudoke. The Kudoke 1 is Stefan Kudokes first flagship model, this fitted with a simple yet stunning silver dial. These watches are extremely desirable, and sell out upon release; there is an alleged 12-month wait list just for the opportunity to be considered to purchase one. This Kudoke 1 remains unworn/new, and comes complete with its original Kudoke boxes and paperwork, as sold new.

title: Vacheron Constantin “LNIB 4020T” Traditionnelle
description: Up for grabs is a breathtaking like-new, relatively unworn Vacheron Constantin Traditionnelle, reference 4020T, sold in 2022. This happens to be one of our favorite models from Vacheron, and features a skeletonized triple-date calendar with moonphase, housed in 18kt rose gold. This watch comes with its original VC Warranty card, booklet, and box.

title: MB&F Horological Machine HM7″AquaPod”
description: Up for grabs is one of MB&Fs most unique creations, the Horological Machine N°7 AquaPod, in its rarest iteration — Platinum Red, limited to just 25 pieces worldwide. Inspired by the jellyfish, the AquaPod is built around a radially symmetric, concentric architecture with a 60-second flying tourbillon crowning the movement under a massive domed sapphire crystal. Powered by a solid titanium tentacle-like automatic winding rotor, with a red sapphire crystal unidirectional rotating bezel — the first

title: MB&F Legacy Machine Perpetual
description: Offered here is MB&Fs flagship model of the Legacy Machine line the Legacy Machine perpetual calendar, that was done in collaboration with Irish independent watchmaker Stephen McDonnell. Visually, the LM Perpetual is unmistakable with its large, suspended balance wheel floating above the dial, with a striking perpetual calendar mechanism displayed in a three-dimensional layout. Originally launched in 2015, this watch at that time was MB&Fs most complicated model with 581 components. This particu

title: MB&F Horological Machine HM8 “Can-AM”
description: Offered here is one of MB&Fs avant-garde timepieces with a striking, unconventional design that exemplifies MB&Fs commitment to pushing the boundaries of traditional watchmaking. The HM8 was given the moniker “Can-Am”, an automotive inspired timepiece with a design inspired by automotive parts such as a cars suspension, and the time transmitted on its side as a drivers watch with its speedometer-type display. This particular example embodies the brand’s philosophy of kinetic sculpture on the wri

title: Audemars Piguet “5043BA” Square Watch
description: Up for grabs is a cool and rare Audemars Piguet square watch in yellow gold, given the reference 5034BA. This particular watch, according to an Extract of the Archives of Audemars Piguet, was produced in May of 1963. The condition of this quirky vintage Audemars Piguet is great, retaining a strong case with a crisp hallmark on the underside of the lugs, and a well-preserved dial, showing light wear over the years.

title: Omega “145.022-69BA” Speedmaster
description: Up for grabs is a 1969 Omega Speedmaster in 18kt yellow gold (reference 145.022-69BA). These yellow gold speedmasters were a limited production piece with only 1,014 watches ever produced. The first 32 watches were gifted to astronauts, politicians, and other personalities. The other examples were sold to the public and numbered the present example was numbered 902, and originally delivered to Mexico in 1970 according to an Extract from the Archives of Omega (included in the sale). This gold Ome

title: Eberhard “Gilt” Scafograf 300 Diver
description: Up for grabs is a rare and highly sought-after Eberhard Scafograf 300, with uncommon gilt inlay text, produced in the mid-1960s. This example is in amazing vintage condition. The twisted-lugs case does not show signs of polishing, and still has defined bevels in the lugs. The dial is still very glossy with minimal wear from aging. The bezel shows minimal wear, overall very well preserved. Further, the dial still retains its original lume, aged to a nice golden yellow, and matching in the hands.

title: Omega “Full-Set Tintin” Speedmaster
description: While not quite vintage (yet), this 2015 Omega Speedmaster Tintin, so nicknamed for its color scheme and would-be collaboration with the famous comic The Adventures of Tintin, draws heavy inspiration from the racing-dial Speedmasters of the past. This particular Omega Speedmaster remains in nice used condition, showing light wear from use over the years. Further, it remains complete, with its original Omega Speedmaster box, Warranty Cards, and booklet.

title: Audemars Piguet “Full-Set Kasparov 25960BA” Royal Oak Chronograph
description: Up for grabs is a beautiful Audemars Piguet Royal Oak chronograph, circa 2004, with the reference 25960BA (Yellow Gold), and fitted with a white dial. This watch has been nicknamed the Kasparov due to the famous chess Grandmaster Garry Kasparov, who would wear this model during his professional games, and would always remove it from his wrist prior to his last, winning moves. This Audemars Piguet Royal Oak chronograph remains in excellent vintage condition, remaining unpolished and sharp, and se

title: Roger Dubuis “Full-Set H40” Calendar Watch
description: Offered here is a lovely Roger Dubuis, circa 1990s, from their Homage collection, reference H40.5749.0, in 18kt white gold. This particular example was built as an automatic triple-date calendar watch with observatory chronometer certifications. This watch comes super complete, with its original Roger Dubuis box, two Warranty Certificates (dating to 1999), Besancon Observatory Timing Certificate, extra solid caseback, and original hangtag, and matching RD white gold tang buckle. The watch itself

title: Rolex “Full-Set 1665 Double Red” Sea-Dweller
description: Up for grabs is a beautiful Rolex Double Red Seadweller, circa 1977, at the very end of production of the Double Reds. This example has a Mark IV (MK4) dial. These watches were designed for diving under extreme depths, and were thus fitted with a helium escape valve on the left-hand side of the case, which was used to release pressure as it built up under water. This particular example comes complete as a full-set, with original Rolex box (with 1665 sticker on the side of the box), punched Warra

title: Audemars Piguet “14840BA” Royal Oak
description: Up for grabs is an ultra-rare Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, produced in 1994, and given the reference 14840BA. This reference is a factory bejeweled extension of the mid-size 14790 line, featuring factory diamonds on the bezel, bracelet, and baguette-cut stones on the center links. Further, this particular example is potentially unique, fit with a factory Mother-of-Pearl dial, accented by yet more diamonds for the markers. This example comes with an AP Digital Extract of the Archives, confirming it

title: Audemars Piguet “25831PT Anniversary Tourbillon” Royal Oak
description: Offered here is an exceedingly rare Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, reference 25831PT, produced circa 1997, to commemorate the 25th anniversary of the Royal Oak. According to Audemars Piguet, only 5 of these were made in platinum, making this one of the lowest production Royal Oaks made. This model was housed in a 40mm platinum case, and fit with a Caliber 2875 movement, featuring tourbillon, power reserve, and day of the month complications. Further, the watch is fit with a guilloche blue dial, whic

title: Audemars Piguet “25718OR Tourbillon” Jules Audemars
description: Offered for sale is a very rare Audemars Piguet Tourbillon watch from their Jules Audemars Reserve de Marche line, reference 25718OR, circa 1990s, housed in rose gold case with matching rose gold dial. This model is fitted in a 39mm case, and features a date, power reserve (reserve de marche) and tourbillon complications. The watch itself is in great vintage condition, unpolished with minor signs of wear. The watch comes with its original Audemars Piguet matching tang buckle.

title: Omega “CK2077 Multi-Scale Sector” Chronograph
description: Up for grabs is an insanely rare and early chronograph from Omega, reference CK2077, circa 1943, powered by the legendary 33.3CHRO movement. The reference 2077 is adorned by collectors, as they were the first waterproof chronographs made by Omega, fitted in a large 38mm screwback case with pump pushers. This particular example was originally delivered to Switzerland, and is fitted with a black gilt tri-color dial (purple, silver and black) with both tachy and telemeter scales. This dial variant

title: IWC “Full-Set IW322701” Ingenieur
description: Up for grabs is an IWC Ingenieur black dial, in stainless steel. The Ingenieur line, a collection originally from the 1950s as an anti-magnetic watch for engineers and scientists is IWCs marquee integrated bracelet sports watch. This particular Ingenieur is in like great used condition, carefully worn a handful of times by the original owner. The watch comes complete with its original IWC box, Warranty Card, and all accessories as new including an extra leather strap.

title: Tag Heuer “BNIB Fragment Design” Formula 1
description: Offered here is a special edition Tag Heuer Carrera, made in collaboration with Fragment Design, released in 2020. Limited to 500 pieces, this watch is a modern take on vintage Heuer designs, particularly the Viceroy Autavia, featuring a steel bracelet and Fragments iconic lightning bolt logo. This example is brand new, never worn or sized, and complete with its original Tag Heuer box, warranty card, and all accessories.

title: Tornek Rayville “No. 2” TR-900
description: After the success by Blancpain with their Fifty Fathoms diver, and their universal acceptance by the Naval Unit of countries across the globe, a man by the name of Allen Tornek proposed that the US Navy also order and make use of the Fifty Fathoms dive watches in service. However, due to import restrictions (the US Government would only award contracts to those products produced in the USA), Blancpain was unable to sell the Fifty Fathoms to the US Navy. As such, Tornek sourced and modified movem

title: Audemars Piguet “25902PT Skeleton Tourbillon” Royal Oak
description: Offered here is an exceedingly rare Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, reference 25902PT, produced circa early 2000s. According to Audemars Piguet, only approximately 11 pieces of this reference were made over a 10 year period, of which 6 were in platinum, making this one of the lowest production Royal Oaks made. This model was housed in a 40mm platinum case, and fit with a Caliber 2875 movement, featuring tourbillon, power reserve, and day of the month complications. Further, the watch is enhanced by i

title: Rolex “1803 Rose Gold Arabic” Day-Date
description: Up for grabs is a breathtaking and incredibly rare Rolex Day-Date, reference 1803, circa 1960. This particular Day-Date was produced in 18kt rose gold, and made specifically for the Arab/Arabic market, fit with an Arabic dial and day/date disks. Very few of these special Day-Dates are known to exist, and very rarely come to market. This particular example may be the best example of this configuration known and was discovered fresh-to-market last year in a small jewelry shop in upstate New York.

title: Blancpain “Demo MilSpec 1” Fifty Fathoms
description: In the early 1950s, an elite unit of the French Navy, responsible for their own diving equipment and not satisfied with any of the current diving watches on the market, set out to make their own watch that they deemed suitable for diving, known as the Frogmen. The result was an inevitable partnership with Blancpain for the creation of the watch we know as the Fifty Fathoms. Although initially created for the French Navy, these military specification (Milspec) watches were ordered by other milita

title: Rolex “Full-Set 18388 Pave & Ruby” Day-Date
description: Up for grabs is a stunning and very rare Rolex Day-Date, reference 18388, circa 1994, with a factory pave diamond and ruby dial, together with diamond-set in the bezel and lugs. These factory diamond and bejeweled watches were considered Rolexs top of the line and ornate offerings, and remain to this day, usually reserved for top clients and not easily ordered by the general public. This particular example comes complete with its original Rolex Presidential box and punched Warranty Certificate.

title: Audemars Piguet “25980AI Concept” Royal Oak Offshore Skeleton
description: Offered here is an unusual Royal Oak Offshore concept, housed in titanium and 44mm, reference 25980AI.00.D003su.01. The Concept line is Audemars Piguets attempt at a mixture of haute horology of the highest order, mixed with a futuristic, avant-garde style. The watch itself is in used condition, with some wear on the case, bezel and buckle, his particular example comes watch only.

title: Rolex “Salmon 2508” Chronograph
description: Up for grabs is a stunning and rare steel Rolex chronograph, the reference 2508, circa 1937. This particular 2508 is a second generation example, denoted by its 35mm case with square button pushers. Further, this example features a lovely salmon-color sector-snail dial, with both telemeter and tachymetre scales. This Rolex chronograph is in good vintage condition. The case has been previously polished, but lightly, with thick and even lugs. The original dial shows aging/wear, consistent with the

title: Patek Philippe “5168G Green” Aquanaut
description: Up for grabs is a 2019 Patek Philippe Aquanaut, reference 5168G, housed in 18kt white gold, and fitted with a green khaki dial and matching strap. This Patek Philippe Aquanaut remains in excellent pre-owned condition, with light signs of wear from use; the strap has been sized to fit an approximate 7 wrist. The watch comes complete as sold new, with its original Patek Philippe boxes, Certificate of Origin, and all accompanying Patek booklets.

title: Rolex “Full-Set 1665 Double Red” Sea-Dweller
description: Up for grabs is a beautiful Rolex Double Red Seadweller, circa 1977, at the very end of production of the Double Reds. This example has a Mark IV (MK4) dial. These watches were designed for diving under extreme depths, and were thus fitted with a helium escape valve on the left-hand side of the case, which was used to release pressure as it built up under water.
